summary.summary: Adds a dedicated NumberFormatter (basalFormatter) to TherapySettingEditorView and uses it to format .unitPerHour values. The formatter sets minimumFractionDigits = 2 and maximumFractionDigits = 3. The .unitPerHour case in the value formatting switch was moved to return basalFormatter.string(from: decimalValue as NSNumber) instead of falling through to the prior decimalValue.description path. Changes are in Trio/Sources/Modules/Onboarding/View/TherapySettingEditorView.swift.
summary.userFacingImpact: Displayed basal rates for the unitPerHour unit in the onboarding TherapySettingEditorView will now be formatted with a minimum of 2 and a maximum of 3 fractional digits (using the new basalFormatter) instead of using decimalValue.description. This will change the visual rounding/precision for small basal steps; the diff does not show other behavioral changes, but a human review may be desired to confirm no unintended side effects in other code paths.
